source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
What is the perimeter of the star (in centimetres) if you know that the star on the picture is formed by four equal circles with radius $5 \mathrm{~cm}$, one square and four equilateral triangles?
<image1>

image_dependency: Question explicitly refers to 'the star in the picture'
The arrangement of the four circles, square, and equilateral triangles is not fully specified in text, so the perimeter depends on the diagram
multi_step: Finding the perimeter of a composite figure typically requires identifying which parts of circles and polygons lie on the outer boundary
The figure combines multiple shape types: four equal circles, one square, and four equilateral triangles
The solver likely must translate the given radius into side/arc contributions and then sum them
No multiple-choice shortcuts are provided (choices is empty)
verifiability: answer_text='160' is a clear single target value
registry_passed=true and both image and answer are present
A geometry-based perimeter computation can be checked against the diagram and stated radius

risk_flags: exact boundary interpretation may rely on visual layout not fully specified in text

decision_reasons: Core assets are complete: question, image, and numeric answer are all present
asset_registry_record has no issues and registry_passed=true
The sample has high verifiability (0.92) with a clear single numeric target answer
Although the problem is strongly diagram-dependent, the required image is available, so the core semantics are recoverable
